meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Mastering User Onboarding for SaaS Apps
content.seo_description: Learn to implement professional user onboarding to overcome the 'tutorial hell' plateau. A practical case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To break through the plateau of basic apps, you must master user onboarding. By implementing a guided, data-driven flow using Antigravity for state management and Claude Code for rapid iteration, you transform a static interface into an interactive experience that drives user retention and showcases your engineering maturity.
content.intro:
You have built the to-do list, the weather app, and maybe even a basic CRUD dashboard. Yet, you feel stuck in 'tutorial hell,' unable to bridge the gap between a functional prototype and a professional product. The difference lies not in the complexity of your backend, but in how you welcome your users.

Great apps don't just exist; they guide. By mastering user onboarding, you transition from a coder who writes functions to an architect who designs experiences. This case study will show you how to move beyond basic UI, leveraging modern AI-assisted workflows to build a polished, production-ready onboarding flow that will make your portfolio stand out to recruiters and founders alike.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Identify the one action that proves your app's value. We define this to ensure the onboarding flow is focused on getting the user to that specific point as fast as possible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the core value proposition of my SaaS app and suggest a 3-step 'Aha!' sequence that minimizes friction for a first-time user.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Draft an onboarding state machine configuration based on the identified Aha! moment'.

steps.title: Architect the Onboarding State Machine
steps.description: Use a state machine to manage the onboarding flow, preventing users from getting stuck in invalid UI states.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust state machine schema for a user onboarding process, handling states: loading, tutorial_step_1, tutorial_step_2, and completion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add-feature --file src/lib/onboardingState.ts --logic 'Implement state machine with xstate or simple reducer logic'.

steps.title: Build Contextual Tooltips
steps.description: Implement non-intrusive, context-aware tooltips that highlight key features without overwhelming the user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable React component for a contextual tooltip that triggers based on the current onboarding state managed by our state machine.

steps.title: Implement Progress Persistence
steps.description: Ensure that if a user refreshes or leaves, their onboarding progress is saved to the backend or local storage.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a hook that syncs the current onboarding state to localStorage and triggers an API call to update user profile status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude refactor --file src/hooks/useOnboarding.ts --add 'persistence logic to localStorage and backend sync'.

steps.title: QA and Edge Case Testing
steps.description: Test the flow against common user errors, such as refreshing mid-flow or clicking back buttons unexpectedly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: List 5 critical edge cases for a multi-step user onboarding flow and provide unit tests to cover them using Vitest.

pro_tips: Never force a sign-up before the first 'Aha!' moment; let them experience the value first.
Use 'Progressive Disclosure'—only show advanced settings after the user has successfully completed the basic onboarding flow.
Instrument your onboarding flow with telemetry to see exactly where users drop off.
Keep your onboarding copy human and conversational, avoiding technical jargon at all costs.
Always provide a 'Skip' button; users hate being trapped in forced tutorials.
resources: UX Design Principles for SaaS: https://nngroup.com
content.next_steps: Deploy your onboarding flow to a staging environment, run a usability test with three friends, and document the drop-off metrics to iterate.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to turn any basic project into a professional-grade SaaS product. Stop building for yourself and start building for the user. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
